categoryJustifications.permissions: Inherent risk costs applied for high-risk declarativeNetRequest and medium-risk activeTab and clipboardWrite. All permissions are strictly aligned with the functional requirements of a web clipper, with no evidence of over-privileging.
categoryJustifications.contentScripts: Broad injection scope (all HTTP/HTTPS) is functionally necessary for a clipper to operate on any page, but it creates a significant inherent attack surface for reading page content.
categoryJustifications.webAccessibleResources: The use of a global wildcard (*://*/*) for web-accessible resources is overly broad, increasing the risk of browser fingerprinting by malicious websites.
categoryJustifications.contentSecurityPolicy: No custom CSP is defined, which in a modern context (MV3) defaults to a restrictive policy that prevents the execution of remote scripts and unsafe-eval.
categoryJustifications.externallyConnectable: No external connectivity is declared, effectively closing the extension off from unsolicited messaging attacks from arbitrary websites.

reasoning.text: The extension's permissions, including activeTab and contextMenus, are perfectly aligned with its stated purpose of clipping web content.
High user trust signal with over 400,000 users and a 4.8 rating suggests a reliable and well-maintained tool.
The absence of 'cookies' or 'history' permissions significantly limits the extension's ability to track user behavior across sessions.
The use of declarativeNetRequest is a high-privilege API, but it is the modern, safer standard for network interaction in MV3 extensions.
Broad content script injection on all websites is a functional necessity for this tool but means the extension can technically read any page you visit while active.
Web Accessible Resources are configured with a broad wildcard, which is a minor security hygiene concern regarding browser fingerprinting.
No externally_connectable domains are defined, which prevents malicious websites from attempting to exploit the extension's internal APIs.

whatCanBeCollected: Full page content and HTML structure of the active tab for clipping.
Text or data copied to the clipboard via the clipboardWrite permission.
The URL and title of the current website being visited.
User-defined settings and clipping templates stored locally via the storage API.

capabilities: Modify or block network requests to clean headers or handle redirects during clipping.
Inject user interface elements directly into web pages for highlighting and saving.
Add custom interaction points to the browser's right-click context menu.
Programmatically trigger clipboard actions to save snippets to the user's device.

security: Only use the clipping feature on websites where you trust the content.
Ensure your Obsidian desktop application is kept up to date to match extension security standards.
Be cautious when clipping pages that contain sensitive personal or financial information.
privacy: Review the extension's storage settings to see what metadata is being saved with your clips.
Check your Obsidian sync settings to understand where your clipped data is being uploaded.
usage: Watch for the extension requesting new, unrelated permissions like 'identity' or 'history' in future updates.
Monitor for any unexpected changes to website headers or network behavior when the extension is active.
